primary_sources.observed: SQLite-backed Durable Object storage is transactional and strongly consistent; methods are atomically isolated, SQL supports primary keys, ON CONFLICT behavior, and storage transaction APIs. Documentation last updated 2026-05-27.
GitHub Actions OIDC tokens expose invocation claims including issuer, expiration, run identifiers, and JWT ID for downstream authorization and replay classification.
The JWT ID claim is a unique token identifier and can be used to prevent replay.

finding: Replay acceptance must be one atomic uniqueness decision, not a separate read followed by write. A SQLite PRIMARY KEY on the privacy-preserving replay_key plus INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING produces exactly one winner for a retained issuer-jti digest.
design_implication: Back the R-005 replay evaluator with one SQLite-backed Durable Object namespace. Route all replay decisions to the same object identity and execute the unique insert synchronously before any deployment or evidence promotion.

cases: schema contains replay_key primary key and expiry index
32 concurrent submissions produce exactly one accepted result and 31 replay rejections
expired retained state is pruned before a renewed key is accepted
already expired token is rejected without insertion

falsification_route: Deploy the adapter against a real SQLite-backed Durable Object, send at least two simultaneous requests with the same replay_key to the same object identity, and reject the design if more than one response is accepted, if a raw JWT or jti is persisted, or if deployment/evidence promotion can occur before the insert result.
limits: The concurrency test uses a deterministic in-memory SQL adapter, not the Cloudflare runtime.
The adapter is not yet wired into the Cloudflare deployment workflow.
Correctness depends on routing all decisions for the protected replay domain to the same Durable Object identity.
Replay rejection does not establish signature validity, workflow authorization, deployment success, or evidence truth.
